fields.name: ReqMessageId
fields.description: Message ID to request. Supports any message in this 10000-10099 range

10007.description: Control message with all data sent in UCP control message.

fields.name: flight_id
fields.description: Flight Identification: 8 ASCII characters, '0' through '9', 'A' through 'Z' or space. Spaces (0x20) used as a trailing pad character, or when call sign is unavailable.

fields.name: baroAltMSL

10008.description: Status message with information from UCP Heartbeat and Status messages.

fields.name: NIC_NACp
fields.description: Integrity and Accuracy of traffic reported as a 4-bit value for each field (NACp 7:4, NIC 3:0) and encoded by Containment Radius (HPL) and Estimated Position Uncertainty (HFOM), respectively

fields.name: flight_id
fields.description: Flight Identification: 8 ASCII characters, '0' through '9', 'A' through 'Z' or space. Spaces (0x20) used as a trailing pad character, or when call sign is unavailable.

10151.description: Composite EFI and Governor data from Loweheiser equipment.  This message is created by the EFI unit based on its own data and data received from a governor attached to that EFI unit.

fields.name: curr_rot
fields.description: Load current being consumed by the UAV (sum of curr_gen and curr_batt)

fields.name: runtime
fields.description: Seconds this generator has run since it was rebooted.

fields.name: until_maintenance
fields.description: Seconds until this generator requires maintenance.  A negative value indicates maintenance is past due.

fields.name: generator_temp
fields.description: The temperature of the mechanical motor, fuel cell core or generator.

fields.name: busname
fields.description: Name of device on bus (for SPI).

fields.name: result
fields.description: 0 for success, anything else is failure code.

fields.name: busname
fields.description: Name of device on bus (for SPI).

fields.name: result
fields.description: 0 for success, anything else is failure code.

11004.description: Send a secure command. Data should be signed with a private key corresponding with a public key known to the recipient. Signature should be over the concatenation of the sequence number (little-endian format), the operation (little-endian format) the data and the session key. For SECURE_COMMAND_GET_SESSION_KEY the session key should be zero length. The data array consists of the data followed by the signature. The sum of the data_length and the sig_length cannot be more than 220. The format of the data is command specific.

11011.description: Camera vision based attitude and position deltas.

fields.name: angle_delta
fields.description: Defines a rotation vector [roll, pitch, yaw] to the current MAV_FRAME_BODY_FRD from the previous MAV_FRAME_BODY_FRD.

fields.name: position_delta
fields.description: Change in position to the current MAV_FRAME_BODY_FRD from the previous FRAME_BODY_FRD rotated to the current MAV_FRAME_BODY_FRD.

fields.name: time_usec
fields.description: Timestamp (synced to UNIX time or since system boot).

fields.name: time_delta_usec
fields.description: Time since the last reported camera frame.

11020.description: Angle of Attack and Side Slip Angle.

11030.description: ESC Telemetry Data for ESCs 1 to 4, matching data sent by BLHeli ESCs.

fields.name: count
fields.description: count of telemetry packets received (wraps at 65535).

11031.description: ESC Telemetry Data for ESCs 5 to 8, matching data sent by BLHeli ESCs.

fields.name: count
fields.description: count of telemetry packets received (wraps at 65535).

11032.description: ESC Telemetry Data for ESCs 9 to 12, matching data sent by BLHeli ESCs.

fields.name: count
fields.description: count of telemetry packets received (wraps at 65535).

fields.name: param_id
fields.description: Onboard parameter id, terminated by NULL if the length is less than 16 human-readable chars and WITHOUT null termination (NULL) byte if the length is exactly 16 chars - applications have to provide 16+1 bytes storage if the ID is stored as string

11035.description: Read a configured an OSD parameter slot.

fields.name: param_id
fields.description: Onboard parameter id, terminated by NULL if the length is less than 16 human-readable chars and WITHOUT null termination (NULL) byte if the length is exactly 16 chars - applications have to provide 16+1 bytes storage if the ID is stored as string

fields.name: sensor_type
fields.description: Class id of the distance sensor type.

fields.name: obstacle_id
fields.description: Unique ID given to each obstacle so that its movement can be tracked. Use UINT16_MAX if object ID is unknown or cannot be determined.

fields.name: min_distance
fields.description: Minimum distance the sensor can measure.

fields.name: max_distance
fields.description: Maximum distance the sensor can measure.

11039.description: The MCU status, giving MCU temperature and voltage. The min and max voltages are to allow for detecting power supply instability.

11040.description: ESC Telemetry Data for ESCs 13 to 16, matching data sent by BLHeli ESCs.

11041.description: ESC Telemetry Data for ESCs 17 to 20, matching data sent by BLHeli ESCs.

11043.description: ESC Telemetry Data for ESCs 25 to 28, matching data sent by BLHeli ESCs.

11044.description: ESC Telemetry Data for ESCs 29 to 32, matching data sent by BLHeli ESCs.

fields.name: count
fields.description: count of telemetry packets received (wraps at 65535).

11060.description: Send a key-value pair as string. The use of this message is discouraged for normal packets, but a quite efficient way for testing new messages and getting experimental debug output.

12900.description: Data for filling the OpenDroneID Basic ID message. This and the below messages are primarily meant for feeding data to/from an OpenDroneID implementation. E.g. https://github.com/opendroneid/opendroneid-core-c. These messages are compatible with the ASTM F3411 Remote ID standard and the ASD-STAN prEN 4709-002 Direct Remote ID standard. Additional information and usage of these messages is documented at https://mavlink.io/en/services/opendroneid.html.

fields.name: id_or_mac
fields.description: Only used for drone ID data received from other UAs. See detailed description at https://mavlink.io/en/services/opendroneid.html.

fields.name: id_type
fields.description: Indicates the format for the uas_id field of this message.

fields.name: ua_type
fields.description: Indicates the type of UA (Unmanned Aircraft).

fields.name: uas_id
fields.description: UAS (Unmanned Aircraft System) ID following the format specified by id_type. Shall be filled with nulls in the unused portion of the field.

12901.description: Data for filling the OpenDroneID Location message. The float data types are 32-bit IEEE 754. The Location message provides the location, altitude, direction and speed of the aircraft.

fields.name: id_or_mac
fields.description: Only used for drone ID data received from other UAs. See detailed description at https://mavlink.io/en/services/opendroneid.html.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Indicates whether the unmanned aircraft is on the ground or in the air.

fields.name: height_reference
fields.description: Indicates the reference point for the height field.

fields.name: horizontal_accuracy
fields.description: The accuracy of the horizontal position.

fields.name: vertical_accuracy
fields.description: The accuracy of the vertical position.

fields.name: barometer_accuracy
fields.description: The accuracy of the barometric altitude.

fields.name: speed_accuracy
fields.description: The accuracy of the horizontal and vertical speed.

fields.name: direction
fields.description: Direction over ground (not heading, but direction of movement) measured clockwise from true North: 0 - 35999 centi-degrees. If unknown: 36100 centi-degrees.

fields.name: speed_horizontal
fields.description: Ground speed. Positive only. If unknown: 25500 cm/s. If speed is larger than 25425 cm/s, use 25425 cm/s.

fields.name: speed_vertical
fields.description: The vertical speed. Up is positive. If unknown: 6300 cm/s. If speed is larger than 6200 cm/s, use 6200 cm/s. If lower than -6200 cm/s, use -6200 cm/s.

fields.name: latitude
fields.description: Current latitude of the unmanned aircraft. If unknown: 0 (both Lat/Lon).

fields.name: longitude
fields.description: Current longitude of the unmanned aircraft. If unknown: 0 (both Lat/Lon).

fields.name: altitude_barometric
fields.description: The altitude calculated from the barometric pressure. Reference is against 29.92inHg or 1013.2mb. If unknown: -1000 m.

fields.name: altitude_geodetic
fields.description: The geodetic altitude as defined by WGS84. If unknown: -1000 m.

fields.name: height
fields.description: The current height of the unmanned aircraft above the take-off location or the ground as indicated by height_reference. If unknown: -1000 m.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: Seconds after the full hour with reference to UTC time. Typically the GPS outputs a time-of-week value in milliseconds. First convert that to UTC and then convert for this field using ((float) (time_week_ms % (60*60*1000))) / 1000. If unknown: 0xFFFF.

12902.description: Data for filling the OpenDroneID Authentication message. The Authentication Message defines a field that can provide a means of authenticity for the identity of the UAS (Unmanned Aircraft System). The Authentication message can have two different formats. For data page 0, the fields PageCount, Length and TimeStamp are present and AuthData is only 17 bytes. For data page 1 through 15, PageCount, Length and TimeStamp are not present and the size of AuthData is 23 bytes.

fields.name: id_or_mac
fields.description: Only used for drone ID data received from other UAs. See detailed description at https://mavlink.io/en/services/opendroneid.html.

fields.name: last_page_index
fields.description: This field is only present for page 0. Allowed range is 0 - 15. See the description of struct ODID_Auth_data at https://github.com/opendroneid/opendroneid-core-c/blob/master/libopendroneid/opendroneid.h.

fields.name: length
fields.description: This field is only present for page 0. Total bytes of authentication_data from all data pages. See the description of struct ODID_Auth_data at https://github.com/opendroneid/opendroneid-core-c/blob/master/libopendroneid/opendroneid.h.

fields.name: authentication_data
fields.description: Opaque authentication data. For page 0, the size is only 17 bytes. For other pages, the size is 23 bytes. Shall be filled with nulls in the unused portion of the field.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: This field is only present for page 0. 32 bit Unix Timestamp in seconds since 00:00:00 01/01/2019.

12903.description: Data for filling the OpenDroneID Self ID message. The Self ID Message is an opportunity for the operator to (optionally) declare their identity and purpose of the flight. This message can provide additional information that could reduce the threat profile of a UA (Unmanned Aircraft) flying in a particular area or manner. This message can also be used to provide optional additional clarification in an emergency/remote ID system failure situation.

fields.name: id_or_mac
fields.description: Only used for drone ID data received from other UAs. See detailed description at https://mavlink.io/en/services/opendroneid.html.

fields.name: description_type
fields.description: Indicates the type of the description field.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Text description or numeric value expressed as ASCII characters. Shall be filled with nulls in the unused portion of the field.

12904.description: Data for filling the OpenDroneID System message. The System Message contains general system information including the operator location/altitude and possible aircraft group and/or category/class information.

fields.name: id_or_mac
fields.description: Only used for drone ID data received from other UAs. See detailed description at https://mavlink.io/en/services/opendroneid.html.

fields.name: classification_type
fields.description: Specifies the classification type of the UA.

fields.name: category_eu
fields.description: When classification_type is MAV_ODID_CLASSIFICATION_TYPE_EU, specifies the category of the UA.

fields.name: class_eu
fields.description: When classification_type is MAV_ODID_CLASSIFICATION_TYPE_EU, specifies the class of the UA.

fields.name: area_count
fields.description: Number of aircraft in the area, group or formation (default 1). Used only for swarms/multiple UA.

fields.name: area_radius
fields.description: Radius of the cylindrical area of the group or formation (default 0). Used only for swarms/multiple UA.

fields.name: operator_latitude
fields.description: Latitude of the operator. If unknown: 0 (both Lat/Lon).

fields.name: operator_longitude
fields.description: Longitude of the operator. If unknown: 0 (both Lat/Lon).

fields.name: area_ceiling
fields.description: Area Operations Ceiling relative to WGS84. If unknown: -1000 m. Used only for swarms/multiple UA.

fields.name: area_floor
fields.description: Area Operations Floor relative to WGS84. If unknown: -1000 m. Used only for swarms/multiple UA.

fields.name: operator_altitude_geo
fields.description: Geodetic altitude of the operator relative to WGS84. If unknown: -1000 m.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: 32 bit Unix Timestamp in seconds since 00:00:00 01/01/2019.

12905.description: Data for filling the OpenDroneID Operator ID message, which contains the CAA (Civil Aviation Authority) issued operator ID.

fields.name: id_or_mac
fields.description: Only used for drone ID data received from other UAs. See detailed description at https://mavlink.io/en/services/opendroneid.html.

fields.name: operator_id_type
fields.description: Indicates the type of the operator_id field.

fields.name: operator_id
fields.description: Text description or numeric value expressed as ASCII characters. Shall be filled with nulls in the unused portion of the field.

12915.description: An OpenDroneID message pack is a container for multiple encoded OpenDroneID messages (i.e. not in the format given for the above message descriptions but after encoding into the compressed OpenDroneID byte format). Used e.g. when transmitting on Bluetooth 5.0 Long Range/Extended Advertising or on WiFi Neighbor Aware Networking or on WiFi Beacon.

fields.name: id_or_mac
fields.description: Only used for drone ID data received from other UAs. See detailed description at https://mavlink.io/en/services/opendroneid.html.

fields.name: single_message_size
fields.description: This field must currently always be equal to 25 (bytes), since all encoded OpenDroneID messages are specified to have this length.

fields.name: msg_pack_size
fields.description: Number of encoded messages in the pack (not the number of bytes). Allowed range is 1 - 9.

fields.name: messages
fields.description: Concatenation of encoded OpenDroneID messages. Shall be filled with nulls in the unused portion of the field.

12918.description: Transmitter (remote ID system) is enabled and ready to start sending location and other required information. This is streamed by transmitter. A flight controller uses it as a condition to arm.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Status level indicating if arming is allowed.

fields.name: error
fields.description: Text error message, should be empty if status is good to arm. Fill with nulls in unused portion.

12919.description: Update the data in the OPEN_DRONE_ID_SYSTEM message with new location information. This can be sent to update the location information for the operator when no other information in the SYSTEM message has changed. This message allows for efficient operation on radio links which have limited uplink bandwidth while meeting requirements for update frequency of the operator location.

fields.name: operator_latitude
fields.description: Latitude of the operator. If unknown: 0 (both Lat/Lon).

fields.name: operator_longitude
fields.description: Longitude of the operator. If unknown: 0 (both Lat/Lon).

fields.name: operator_altitude_geo
fields.description: Geodetic altitude of the operator relative to WGS84. If unknown: -1000 m.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: 32 bit Unix Timestamp in seconds since 00:00:00 01/01/2019.

42001.description: Kinematic multi bands (track) output from Daidalus

fields.name: camera_id
fields.description: Video Stream ID (1 for first, 2 for second, etc.)

fields.name: uri
fields.description: Video stream URI (TCP or RTSP URI ground station should connect to) or port number (UDP port ground station should listen to).

50004.description: Start firmware update with encapsulated data.

52501.description: Send data about a control axis from a control loading system. This is the primary message for logging data from MARSH_TYPE_CONTROL_LOADING.

fields.name: axis
fields.description: Control axis on which the measurements were taken.

fields.name: force
fields.description: Force applied in the pilot in the direction of movement axis (not gripping force), measured at the position of pilot's third finger (ring). Unit N (Newton), currently not part of mavschema.xsd

52502.description: State report for motion platform used for moving the cockpit with the pilot for motion cueing. This is the primary message for MARSH_TYPE_MOTION_PLATFORM.

fields.name: health
fields.description: Generic system health (error and warning) status.

fields.name: acc_roll
fields.description: Roll acceleration, positive right. Unit rad/s/s, currently not part of mavschema.xsd

fields.name: acc_pitch
fields.description: Pitch acceleration, positive nose up. Unit rad/s/s, currently not part of mavschema.xsd

fields.name: acc_yaw
fields.description: Yaw acceleration, positive right. Unit rad/s/s, currently not part of mavschema.xsd

52503.description: State report specific for eMotion Motion System by Bosch Rexroth B.V. Values applicable to motion platforms in general are sent in MOTION_PLATFORM_STATE with the same timestamp. Actuators are numbered in a clockwise direction when looking from above, starting from the front right. Actuator position is 0 when actuator is in mid-stroke.

fields.name: error_code
fields.description: Error code extracted from motion status.

fields.name: frame_count
fields.description: Number of message as sent by the Motion System.

fields.name: motion_status
fields.description: Motion Status variable as sent by the system.

fields.name: platform_setpoint_x
fields.description: X axis (surge) platform setpoint, positive forward.

fields.name: platform_setpoint_y
fields.description: Y axis (sway) platform setpoint, positive right.

fields.name: platform_setpoint_z
fields.description: Z axis (heave) platform setpoint, positive down.

fields.name: platform_setpoint_pitch
fields.description: Pitch platform setpoint, positive nose up.

fields.name: effect_setpoint_x
fields.description: X axis (surge) special effect setpoint, positive forward.

fields.name: effect_setpoint_y
fields.description: Y axis (sway) special effect setpoint, positive right.

fields.name: effect_setpoint_z
fields.description: Z axis (heave) special effect setpoint, positive down.

fields.name: effect_setpoint_roll
fields.description: Roll special effect setpoint, positive right.

fields.name: effect_setpoint_pitch
fields.description: Pitch special effect setpoint, positive nose up.

fields.name: effect_setpoint_yaw
fields.description: Yaw special effect setpoint, positive right.

52504.description: These values are an extra cue that should be added to accelerations and rotations etc. resulting from aircraft state, with the resulting cue being the sum of the latest aircraft and extra values. An example use case would be a cockpit shaker.

52505.description: Data for tracking of pilot eye gaze. This is the primary message for MARSH_TYPE_EYE_TRACKER.

fields.name: sensor_id
fields.description: Sensor ID, used for identifying the device and/or person tracked. Set to zero if unknown/unused.

fields.name: surface_id
fields.description: Identifier of surface for 2D gaze point, or an identified region when surface point is invalid. Set to zero if unknown/unused.

fields.name: gaze_origin_x
fields.description: X axis of gaze origin point, NaN if unknown. The reference system depends on specific application.

fields.name: gaze_origin_y
fields.description: Y axis of gaze origin point, NaN if unknown. The reference system depends on specific application.

fields.name: gaze_origin_z
fields.description: Z axis of gaze origin point, NaN if unknown. The reference system depends on specific application.

fields.name: gaze_direction_x
fields.description: X axis of gaze direction vector, expected to be normalized to unit magnitude, NaN if unknown. The reference system should match origin point.

fields.name: gaze_direction_y
fields.description: Y axis of gaze direction vector, expected to be normalized to unit magnitude, NaN if unknown. The reference system should match origin point.

fields.name: gaze_direction_z
fields.description: Z axis of gaze direction vector, expected to be normalized to unit magnitude, NaN if unknown. The reference system should match origin point.

fields.name: video_gaze_x
fields.description: Gaze focal point on video feed x value (normalized 0..1, 0 is left, 1 is right), NaN if unknown

fields.name: video_gaze_y
fields.description: Gaze focal point on video feed y value (normalized 0..1, 0 is top, 1 is bottom), NaN if unknown

fields.name: surface_gaze_x
fields.description: Gaze focal point on surface x value (normalized 0..1, 0 is left, 1 is right), NaN if unknown

fields.name: surface_gaze_y
fields.description: Gaze focal point on surface y value (normalized 0..1, 0 is top, 1 is bottom), NaN if unknown

fields.name: wind_x
fields.description: Wind X speed in NED (North,Est, Down). NAN if unknown.

fields.name: wind_y
fields.description: Wind Y speed in NED (North, East, Down). NAN if unknown.

fields.name: wind_correction_angle
fields.description: Correction angle due to wind. NaN if unknown.

60010.description: Information about a gimbal manager. This message should be requested by a ground station using MAV_CMD_REQUEST_MESSAGE. It mirrors some fields of the GIMBAL_DEVICE_INFORMATION message, but not all. If the additional information is desired, also GIMBAL_DEVICE_INFORMATION should be requested.

fields.name: gimbal_id
fields.description: Gimbal ID (component ID or 1-6 for non-MAVLink gimbal) that this gimbal manager is responsible for.

fields.name: device_cap_flags
fields.description: Gimbal device capability flags. Same flags as reported by GIMBAL_DEVICE_INFORMATION. The flag is only 16 bit wide, but stored in 32 bit, for backwards compatibility (high word is zero).

fields.name: roll_min
fields.description: Hardware minimum roll angle (positive: roll to the right). NaN if unknown.

fields.name: roll_max
fields.description: Hardware maximum roll angle (positive: roll to the right). NaN if unknown.

fields.name: pitch_min
fields.description: Hardware minimum pitch/tilt angle (positive: tilt up). NaN if unknown.

fields.name: pitch_max
fields.description: Hardware maximum pitch/tilt angle (positive: tilt up). NaN if unknown.

fields.name: yaw_min
fields.description: Hardware minimum yaw/pan angle (positive: pan to the right, relative to the vehicle/gimbal base). NaN if unknown.

fields.name: yaw_max
fields.description: Hardware maximum yaw/pan angle (positive: pan to the right, relative to the vehicle/gimbal base). NaN if unknown.

60011.description: Message reporting the current status of a gimbal manager. This message should be broadcast at a low regular rate (e.g. 1 Hz, may be increase momentarily to e.g. 5 Hz for a period of 1 sec after a change).

fields.name: gimbal_id
fields.description: Gimbal ID (component ID or 1-6 for non-MAVLink gimbal) that this gimbal manager is responsible for.

fields.name: supervisor
fields.description: Client who is currently supervisor (0 = none).

fields.name: device_flags
fields.description: Gimbal device flags currently applied. Same flags as reported by GIMBAL_DEVICE_ATTITUDE_STATUS.

60012.description: Message to a gimbal manager to control the gimbal attitude. Angles and rates can be set to NaN according to use case. A gimbal device is never to react to this message.

fields.name: gimbal_id
fields.description: Gimbal ID of the gimbal manager to address (component ID or 1-6 for non-MAVLink gimbal, 0 for all gimbals). Send command multiple times for more than one but not all gimbals.

fields.name: client
fields.description: Client which is contacting the gimbal manager (must be set).

fields.name: device_flags
fields.description: Gimbal device flags to be applied (UINT16_MAX to be ignored). Same flags as used in GIMBAL_DEVICE_SET_ATTITUDE.

fields.name: manager_flags
fields.description: Gimbal manager flags to be applied (0 to be ignored).

fields.name: q
fields.description: Quaternion components, w, x, y, z (1 0 0 0 is the null-rotation). Set first element to NaN to be ignored. The frame is determined by the GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_YAW_IN_xxx_FRAME flags.

fields.name: angular_velocity_x
fields.description: X component of angular velocity (positive: roll to the right). NaN to be ignored.

fields.name: angular_velocity_y
fields.description: Y component of angular velocity (positive: tilt up). NaN to be ignored.

fields.name: angular_velocity_z
fields.description: Z component of angular velocity (positive: pan to the right). NaN to be ignored. The frame is determined by the GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_YAW_IN_xxx_FRAME flags.

60013.description: Message to a gimbal manager to control the gimbal tilt and pan angles. Angles and rates can be set to NaN according to use case. A gimbal device is never to react to this message.

fields.name: gimbal_id
fields.description: Gimbal ID of the gimbal manager to address (component ID or 1-6 for non-MAVLink gimbal, 0 for all gimbals). Send command multiple times for more than one but not all gimbals.

fields.name: client
fields.description: Client which is contacting the gimbal manager (must be set).

fields.name: device_flags
fields.description: Gimbal device flags to be applied (UINT16_MAX to be ignored). Same flags as used in GIMBAL_DEVICE_SET_ATTITUDE.

fields.name: manager_flags
fields.description: Gimbal manager flags to be applied (0 to be ignored).

fields.name: pitch
fields.description: Pitch/tilt angle (positive: tilt up). NaN to be ignored.

fields.name: yaw
fields.description: Yaw/pan angle (positive: pan the right). NaN to be ignored. The frame is determined by the GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_YAW_IN_xxx_FRAME flags.

fields.name: pitch_rate
fields.description: Pitch/tilt angular rate (positive: tilt up). NaN to be ignored.

fields.name: yaw_rate
fields.description: Yaw/pan angular rate (positive: pan to the right). NaN to be ignored. The frame is determined by the GIMBAL_DEVICE_FLAGS_YAW_IN_xxx_FRAME flags.

60014.description: Message to a gimbal manager to correct the gimbal roll angle. This message is typically used to manually correct for a tilted horizon in operation. A gimbal device is never to react to this message.

fields.name: gimbal_id
fields.description: Gimbal ID of the gimbal manager to address (component ID or 1-6 for non-MAVLink gimbal, 0 for all gimbals). Send command multiple times for more than one but not all gimbals.

fields.name: client
fields.description: Client which is contacting the gimbal manager (must be set).

fields.name: roll
fields.description: Roll angle (positive to roll to the right).

fields.name: shot_state
fields.description: Current state in the shot. States are specific to the selected shot mode.

60040.description: Frsky SPort passthrough multi packet container.

fields.name: count
fields.description: Number of passthrough packets in this message.

fields.name: packet_buf
fields.description: Passthrough packet buffer. A packet has 6 bytes: uint16_t id + uint32_t data. The array has space for 40 packets.

fields.name: param_array_len
fields.description: Number of onboard parameters in this array.

fields.name: packet_buf
fields.description: Parameters buffer. Contains a series of variable length parameter blocks, one per parameter, with format as specified elsewhere.

fields.name: param_index_first
fields.description: Index of the first onboard parameter in this array.

60045.description:
Radio link statistics for a MAVLink RC receiver or transmitter and other links. Tx: ground-side device, Rx: vehicle-side device.
        The message is normally emitted in regular time intervals upon each actual or expected reception of an over-the-air data packet on the link.
        A MAVLink RC receiver should emit it shortly after it emits a RADIO_RC_CHANNELS message (if it is emitting that message).
        Per default, rssi values are in MAVLink units: 0 represents weakest signal, 254 represents maximum signal, UINT8_MAX represents unknown.
        The RADIO_LINK_STATS_FLAGS_RSSI_DBM flag is set if the rssi units are negative dBm: 1..254 correspond to -1..-254 dBm, 0 represents no reception, UINT8_MAX represents unknown.
        The target_system field should normally be set to the system id of the system the link is connected to, typically the flight controller.
        The target_component field can normally be set to 0, so that all components of the system can receive the message.
        Note: The frequency fields are extensions to ensure that they are located at the end of the serialized payload and subject to MAVLink's trailing-zero trimming.

fields.name: target_system
fields.description: System ID (ID of target system, normally flight controller).

fields.name: rx_LQ_rc
fields.description: Link quality of RC data stream from Tx to Rx. Values: 1..100, 0: no link connection, UINT8_MAX: unknown.

fields.name: rx_LQ_ser
fields.description: Link quality of serial MAVLink data stream from Tx to Rx. Values: 1..100, 0: no link connection, UINT8_MAX: unknown.

fields.name: rx_rssi1
fields.description: Rssi of antenna 1. 0: no reception, UINT8_MAX: unknown.

fields.name: rx_snr1
fields.description: Noise on antenna 1. Radio link dependent. INT8_MAX: unknown.

fields.name: tx_LQ_ser
fields.description: Link quality of serial MAVLink data stream from Rx to Tx. Values: 1..100, 0: no link connection, UINT8_MAX: unknown.

fields.name: tx_rssi1
fields.description: Rssi of antenna 1. 0: no reception. UINT8_MAX: unknown.

fields.name: tx_snr1
fields.description: Noise on antenna 1. Radio link dependent. INT8_MAX: unknown.

fields.name: rx_rssi2
fields.description: Rssi of antenna 2. 0: no reception, UINT8_MAX: use rx_rssi1 if it is known else unknown.

fields.name: rx_snr2
fields.description: Noise on antenna 2. Radio link dependent. INT8_MAX: use rx_snr1 if it is known else unknown.

fields.name: tx_rssi2
fields.description: Rssi of antenna 2. 0: no reception. UINT8_MAX: use tx_rssi1 if it is known else unknown.

fields.name: tx_snr2
fields.description: Noise on antenna 2. Radio link dependent. INT8_MAX: use tx_snr1 if it is known else unknown.

fields.name: frequency1
fields.description: Frequency on antenna1 in Hz. 0: unknown.

fields.name: frequency2
fields.description: Frequency on antenna2 in Hz. 0: unknown.

60046.description:
Radio link information. Tx: ground-side device, Rx: vehicle-side device.
        The values of the fields in this message do normally not or only slowly change with time, and for most times the message can be send at a low rate, like 0.2 Hz.
        If values change then the message should temporarily be send more often to inform the system about the changes.
        The target_system field should normally be set to the system id of the system the link is connected to, typically the flight controller.
        The target_component field can normally be set to 0, so that all components of the system can receive the message.

fields.name: target_system
fields.description: System ID (ID of target system, normally flight controller).

fields.name: mode
fields.description: Operation mode. Radio link dependent. UINT8_MAX: ignore/unknown.

fields.name: tx_power
fields.description: Tx transmit power in dBm. INT8_MAX: unknown.

fields.name: rx_power
fields.description: Rx transmit power in dBm. INT8_MAX: unknown.

fields.name: mode_str
fields.description: Operation mode as human readable string. Radio link dependent. Terminated by NULL if the string length is less than 6 chars and WITHOUT NULL termination if the length is exactly 6 chars - applications have to provide 6+1 bytes storage if the mode is stored as string. Use a zero-length string if not known.

fields.name: band_str
fields.description: Frequency band as human readable string. Radio link dependent. Terminated by NULL if the string length is less than 6 chars and WITHOUT NULL termination if the length is exactly 6 chars - applications have to provide 6+1 bytes storage if the mode is stored as string. Use a zero-length string if not known.

fields.name: tx_receive_sensitivity
fields.description: Receive sensitivity of Tx in inverted dBm. 1..255 represents -1..-255 dBm, 0: unknown.

fields.name: rx_receive_sensitivity
fields.description: Receive sensitivity of Rx in inverted dBm. 1..255 represents -1..-255 dBm, 0: unknown.

fields.name: tx_frame_rate
fields.description: Frame rate in Hz (frames per second) for Tx to Rx transmission. 0: unknown.

fields.name: rx_frame_rate
fields.description: Frame rate in Hz (frames per second) for Rx to Tx transmission. Normally equal to tx_packet_rate. 0: unknown.

fields.name: tx_ser_data_rate
fields.description: Maximum data rate of serial stream in bytes/s for Tx to Rx transmission. 0: unknown. UINT16_MAX: data rate is 64 KBytes/s or larger.

fields.name: rx_ser_data_rate
fields.description: Maximum data rate of serial stream in bytes/s for Rx to Tx transmission. 0: unknown. UINT16_MAX: data rate is 64 KBytes/s or larger.

60047.description: Injected by a radio link endpoint into the MAVLink stream for purposes of flow control. Should be emitted only by components with component id MAV_COMP_ID_TELEMETRY_RADIO.

fields.name: tx_used_ser_bandwidth
fields.description: Transmit bandwidth consumption. Values: 0..100, UINT8_MAX: invalid/unknown.

fields.name: rx_used_ser_bandwidth
fields.description: Receive bandwidth consumption. Values: 0..100, UINT8_MAX: invalid/unknown.

fields.name: txbuf
fields.description: For compatibility with legacy method. UINT8_MAX: unknown.

fields.name: tx_ser_rate
fields.description: Transmitted bytes per second, UINT16_MAX: invalid/unknown.

fields.name: rx_ser_rate
fields.description: Received bytes per second, UINT16_MAX: invalid/unknown.

fields.name: battery_status
fields.description: Estimated battery run-time without a remote connection and PRS battery voltage

fields.name: alt
fields.description: Altitude (MSL). Note that virtually all GPS modules provide both WGS84 and MSL.

fields.name: ground_alt
fields.description: Altitude above ground, This altitude is measured by a ultrasound, Laser rangefinder or millimeter-wave radar

fields.name: barometer_alt
fields.description: This altitude is measured by a barometer

60052.description: Drone IMU data. Quaternion order is w, x, y, z and a zero rotation would be expressed as (1 0 0 0).
